The pitcher begins releasing pink molten wax into the first jar; a measuring cup and black spoon sit to the right.
A stream of pink wax pours from the pitcher into the first glass jar, partially filling it.
Pink wax continues pouring into the front jar, the level rising around its centered wick.
The first jar fills further with pink wax as the pitcher is held steady above it.
Pink molten wax keeps flowing into the jar, now noticeably filled.
The pour continues, the first jar nearly full of pink wax around the wick.
Wax stream thins as the first jar reaches its fill line.
The pitcher lifts away; the first jar holds a deep pool of pink wax while the other empty jars wait in the row.

seconds_v2.desc: A heat gun is held nozzle-down over the front pink candle in a glass jar, melting its surface; six filled pink jars sit on Standley tissue paper with lids, wick bars and pliers nearby.
The heat gun hovers over the top of a pink candle, smoothing the wax surface with hot air.
Heat gun moves across the jar tops, the pink wax surface glossing and leveling.
The heat gun continues directing hot air onto the candle surface to remove a sinkhole near the wick.
Heat gun held over a jar, the pink wax top reflowing smooth and shiny.
The heat gun keeps warming the candle tops, wax surface settling flat.

windows.embed_text_v2: Smoothing tops of blush-pink soy candles with a black heat gun held above straight-sided glass jars, finishing wax surface, bronze lids black wick-centering clips white wicks and yellow pliers on branded tissue paper
windows.summary_v2: A heat gun is held just above seven freshly poured blush-pink candles in glass jars to smooth and finish their wax tops on branded tissue paper.

tags_v2.action: smoothing candle tops with heat gun
tags_v2.setting: overhead dark wood table with blue LED accent lighting
tags_v2.on_screen_text: Standley (printed on tissue/wrapping paper under the jars)

seconds_v2.desc: Overhead view of seven freshly poured blush-pink candles in straight-sided glass jars arranged on branded 'Standley' tissue paper over a dark wood table; a black heat gun nozzle hovers just above the jars to smooth the wax tops, with bronze lids, black wick-centering bars, white wicks and yellow-handled pliers to the right.
Same arrangement of pink candles; the heat gun is held a few inches above the wax surfaces, gently melting the tops level, blue LED strip glowing along the wall behind the jars.

Heat gun continues hovering over the cluster of pink wax jars; the surfaces of the front candles look glossy and smoothing out, accessories (bronze lids, black centering clips, wicks, pliers) lie on the kraft and tissue paper at right.
The heat gun nozzle shifts slightly over the jars, evenly warming the pink wax tops; seven candles remain set out on the 'Standley' branded paper, finishing the surfaces after pouring.
Continued heat-gun smoothing of the blush-pink candle tops; one front jar's surface shows a small dip near the wick as the warm air levels it, bronze lids and black wick holders staged to the side.
Heat gun held steady above the jar cluster, melting the upper layer of pink wax to remove imperfections; arrangement of jars, lids and tools unchanged on the wood table.
Overhead shot of the pink candles as the heat gun passes over them, the wax tops glistening from the applied heat; blue accent lighting reflects off the glass jars.
Heat gun nozzle remains poised over the front jars, finishing the smooth glossy tops of the pink candles; bronze lids, black centering bars, white wicks and yellow pliers stay grouped on the right.
Final second: the seven blush-pink candles sit with smoothed glossy tops under the hovering heat gun on the 'Standley' tissue paper, completing the heat-gun finishing pass.
